title: Same Tree
description:
Given the roots of two binary trees `p` and `q`, write a function to check if they are the same or not.

Two trees are **the same** if they are structurally identical and nodes have the same value.

**Define a `TreeNode` class** with `val`, `left`, and `right` (defaults `None`). The judge encodes each tree as a **level-order** list (LeetCode style): `null` denotes missing children.
constraints: The number of nodes in both trees is in the range [0, 100]

hint_plan.level_1: Compare roots, then recurse or iterate on left and right subtrees together.
hint_plan.level_2: Base case: both None → true; one None → false; values differ → false.
hint_plan.level_3: Recursive: same_tree(p,q) iff p.val==q.val and same left children and same right children.
hint_plan.level_4: DFS: return same_tree(p.left,q.left) and same_tree(p.right,q.right) after matching values.

canonical_solution_summary: Recursive DFS or iterative DFS/BFS pair traversal.
